questions.question: How many such pairs of letters are there in the word 'RESUMPTION' each of which has as many letters between them in the word as in the English alphabet?
questions.explanation: RESUMPTION 185192113162091514 The pairs are: RU, SP, ON Hence 3 such pairs.

questions.question: Find the odd one out.
Arrange the following words as per the order in dictionary. 1. Tunnel 2. Tumble 3. Turblent 4. Tumult 5. Tumbler
questions.explanation: option (d) is the correct form as per the order in dictionary.

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd word from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: All others except option (a) are chemical compounds, while, oxygen is an element.

questions.question: In the following question, which one set of letters when sequentially placed at the gaps in the given letter series shall complete it? l _ m n _ n _ m l _ m _ l n n _
questions.explanation: l m m n/ l n n m/ l m m n/ l n n m

questions.question: Six people are sitting in a linear row from left to right facing north. K sits towards left of M, who sits towards right of O. L sits to left of N, who is sitting to right of P. O sits to left of P and doesn't sits at any of extreme ends. At most one person sits to right of P. Who sits second to right of O?
questions.explanation: → K sits towards left of M, who sits towards right of O. So, Possibilities are, K O M Or, O K M Also, → L sits to left of N, who is sitting to right of P. So, Possibilities are, L P N Or, P L N Since, → O sits to left of P and doesn't sits at any of extreme ends. So, possible cases are: K O M L P N Or, K O M P L N But, → At most one person sits to right of P. So, 2nd case becomes invalid and, Final arrangement is, K O M L P N Hence, L sits second to right of O.

questions.question: Arrange the given words in a meaningful order. a) Square b) Triangle c) Octagon d) Hexagon e) Heptagon
questions.explanation: The order will be Triangle< Square< Hexagon< Heptagon< Octagon (based on number of sides)

questions.question: If 8 # 20 - 16 = 10 and 16 # 10 - 20 = 8, then in the expression A # 21 - 18 = 42, what is the value of 'A'?
questions.explanation: The logic followed here is: A*21/18 = 42 A = 36

questions.question: In the following question, select the related word from the given alternatives. Kangaroo : Australia ∷ ? : India
questions.explanation: As, Kangaroo is national animal of Australia, similarly, Bengal Tiger is the national animal of India.

questions.question: In the following question, select the related letters from the given alternatives. SGDP : PDSG ∷ WCXI : ?

questions.question: In the following question, select the related number from the given alternatives. 569 : 446 ∷ 348 : ?

In each question below are three statements followed by three conclusions numbered I, II and III. You have to take the three given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the three given statements disregarding commonly known facts. Statements: All benches are umpires. Some umpires are towels. All towels are ties. Conclusions: I. Some towels are benches. II. Some ties are umpires.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/syllogism/image0038374_umpires_benches.jpg) Only II follows

questions.question: Ramesh exits from his house facing north. He walks 10 m straight, takes a left turn & walks 5m, then takes a right turn & walks 10m and finally takes another right turn and walks 50m. Meanwhile Rakesh also leaves his house, walks 5m straight, takes a left turn & walks 20m, then takes a another left turn and walks 50m to meet Ramesh facing each other. What is the distance of Rakesh's house from Ramesh's house and in which direction?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/94250/image012.jpg) So Rakesh's house is 90m to the east of Ramesh's house.

questions.question: If '+' means '÷', '-' means 'x', 'x' means '+' and '÷' means '-', then what will be the value of 11x15-81+27÷18?
questions.explanation: Given expression becomes 11+15x81÷27-18 => 11+45-18 = 38

questions.question: In a certain language, GENERAL is written as MFFGKBQ. How can be PROSTRATE written in that code?
questions.explanation: In the code, GENERAL → NEG-E-LAR → MFF-G-KBQ (consonant -1, vowel +1, middle +2) So, PROSTRATE → SORP-T-ETAR → RPQO-V-FSBQ

questions.question: Select the missing number from the given responses. 345 111929 123364?

A word is represented by only one set of numbers as given in any one of the alternatives. The sets of numbers given in the alternatives are represented by two classes of alphabets as in two matrices given below. The columns and rows of Matrix I are numbered from 0 to 4 and that of Matrix II are numbered from 5 to 9. A letter from these matrices is represented first by its row and next by its column. e.g. 'E' can be represented by 00, 13, 21 etc. and 'M' can be represented by 57, 68, 75 etc. Similarly, you have to identify the set for the word "Cream". MATRIX I 01234 0ENBCR 1RBCEN 2CERBN 3BNERC 4RECNB MATRIX II 56789 5OIMHA 6AHIMO 7MOAIH 8HIMOA 9IAOMH
questions.explanation: From the matrix, we have C → 03, 12, 20, 34 and 42 R → 04, 10, 22, 33 and 40 E → 00, 13, 21, 32 and 41 A → 59, 65, 77, 89 and 96 M → 57, 68, 75, 87 and 98 Only the option 3 has the codes of the letters in the same sequence.

questions.question: Find the missing number in the series. -1, 10, 69, 236, 595, ?

The mirror is placed to the right side of the object. Find the exact mirror image of that object. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image033.png) a) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image034.png) b) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image035.png) c) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image036.png) d) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image037.png)
questions.explanation: Option 1 has bigger vertical line than that of in question figure. Also, bigger and smaller diagonal lines are shown as it is and not a mirror reflection. In option 2, triangle in lower part of the diagram is incomplete and it did not have small vertical line. In option 4, mirror image of both the diagonals is wrong and smaller triangle is misplaced. Option 3 is the correct answer.
